item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting, though I'm leaning at downvote for now due to a few issues, most of which have already been mentioned. I'll really only elaborate on the 2 I feel are most important.

1) You refer to SCP-1078-1 like 9 paragraphs in. "Through a process currently unknown to Foundation researchers, a short period after death, SCP-1078-1 will vanish from the host body and reappear elsewhere"

I got suddenly confused as to what that even was, because you don't mention it at any point previous in the article. You refer to the eye as -1078 the entire time, not 1078-1. Thought maybe there was something else the eye released, like a secondary organism, that was referenced and I somehow missed it. Seems a good idea that if you're going to split the scip into pieces (-1, -2, etc) you should make that clear early in the article.

SCP-1078 refers to -. SCP-1078-1 is --. When implanted in a subject they become an instance of SCP-1078-2.

2) Wall of text is killing my ability to read it. It's a great idea, but I feel that you could tighten up the descriptions without losing any of the content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I groaned when I saw that after that gigantic block of text (adequate paragraph spacing saves it from qualifying as a "wall"), there was an experiment log. This needs to be, like, twice as interesting to justify its length. The item as written doesn't handle that well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm upvoting it because when I saw the name (Sight-Stealing Eye) I thought it was going to be a lot more simplistic.

The description of what it does and the experiment logs make this far more interesting than I initially would have guessed it was going to be

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so tired of implantable anomalous eyeballs.

The only thing stopping me from downvoting is the twist with the very slow erasure, and the bristles integrating with the nervous system in tests. If the rest was written better, I'd upvote.

On reread, this didn't stop me. Sorry. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first thought was that there's already a guy who can't perceive that anyone besides himself exists, but the various other effects of this make it stand out enough on its own, I think. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very interesting. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The subjects were just numbered starting at zero and counting up, not numbered after their experiment. D-1078-7 was used in both experiments 7 and 8, and experiments 9-12 were on animal subjects, so the subject of experiment 13 was the eighth D-Class personnel used, hence why he's D-1078-8.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few bits of spell/grammar/specifics checking aside, I think this is a really solid article. And yes we've had eye-related SCPs before, but if THAT level of similarity was the end-all-be-all of judgement, well then we'd have 173 and NO other statues on the site… And we have quite a few statues.

And yes, me and my sensitive eyes were cringing, especially with the log of the guy who had it implanted *over his own, still-quite-functional eye*. Shudder, man. Shudder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this one's different enough from 1209 to stand on its own. 1209 is a congenital defect common to a particular area that allows/forces the bearers to see through someone else's eye. This one is an artificial eye that implants itself, and may or may not be broadcasting what's being seen, it's location, or anything else to… Somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed a small error:

In the article, you state
However, subject D-1078-8 reported vivid auditory and visual hallucinations; it is believed now that during the late stages of SCP-1078-1's integration, it not only blocks out all stimuli from humans but also creates false stimuli in the mind of its host. Subjects that are not terminated invariably take their own life, either directly out of despair or by desperately trying to wrench SCP-1078-1 out of their eye socket and dying to the resulting cranial hemorrhage. Subject D-1078-8 was prevented from suicide, but after a period of 15 years expired due to [DATA EXPUNGED].

I think you mean D-1078-13? Since D-1078-8 didn't even survive the *implantation* process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this and honestly I think it's sufficiently different from SCP-1209 to remain here. However, the writing feels off in places and I'm having difficulties coming to terms with the wall of text. I think this could be written up a lot more concise (though I won't say I know exactly how or where to edit). No vote for now, but I'll gladly upvote if this gets cleaned up a bit.

Also, congratulations to the author and a promising first article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. One little nitpick: D-1078-2 is a bit young at age 24 for cataract surgery. Also, the word "botched" is a little informal; maybe "failed" surgery or "cataract surgery complicated by infection". Otherwise, very good and upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My bad, thought I saw those terms used in another article and thought that the Foundation used 'em. I'll update those.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When not fully retracted, bristles composed mainly of copper extrude from a 0.25-cm2 area directly opposite the object's "pupil".

When I first read this I though it meant "when the eyeball is not fully retracted". Not sure how to make it clearer. Maybe "When they are not fully retracted"?

male Asiatic

female Negroid

That should be "Asian" and "African", not "Asiatic" and "Negroid".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good, especially for a first article. And even if a similar object exists, I see no reason why we cant have two similar objects if it isnt derivative and theyre both well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like your writing but we already have an SCP like this.

SCP-1209 to be exact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, thanks. :3 Sorry about the voting module, I thought it was something that was automatically on every page, not something that required a block of code. Shall remember in the future!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THE DOWNVOTE HAS BEEN CAST AND THERE IT SHALL REMAIN FOR ALL OF ETERNITY! Not really though. Downvote removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Announcement is now up, I just put up the page first and then went to go announce.
Also, inaugural SCP. Fingers crossed…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tut tut. No voting module and no announcement.
